# Kalebtec Icon Design

> A self-contained agent guide for turning an icon brief into numbered discovery sheets, selected-concept refinements, and a validated production asset package. Use the visual builder at https://icons.kalebtec.com/ or run the same workflow through Codex, Claude Code, or another capable TUI/AUI.

Version: 1.3.1
Updated: 2026-07-14
Repository: https://github.com/kalebteccom/claude-icon-design
License: MIT for the plugin code. Generated brand assets use the license selected for that project.

## Fastest route for an agent UI or terminal UI

Choose the first available route:

1. Codex with the plugin installed: invoke `$icon-design`.
2. Claude Code with the plugin installed: invoke `/icon-design:icon-design`.
3. Any other agent: open https://icons.kalebtec.com/, complete the three brief steps, choose “Another chatbot,” copy the discovery prompt, and paste it into the current conversation.
4. A repository-aware agent can follow this file directly without the plugin.

Recommended opening requests:

```text
Use $icon-design to open the guided brief builder for a new icon project.
```

```text
/icon-design:icon-design
Open the guided brief builder for a new icon project.
```

If the user already supplied a clear brief, skip the builder and begin discovery. If the user pastes a builder export, treat it as the agreed brief and do not ask for the same information again.

## Runtime installation

Codex:

```sh
codex plugin marketplace add kalebteccom/claude-icon-design
codex plugin add icon-design@kalebtec-icon-design
```

Start a new Codex conversation after installation.

Claude Code:

```sh
claude plugin marketplace add kalebteccom/claude-icon-design
claude plugin install icon-design@kalebtec-icon-design
```

Restart Claude Code or run `/reload-plugins` after installation.

## Resolve the bundled skill directory

In the commands below, `<skill-directory>` is the absolute directory containing `SKILL.md`.

- Codex: use the installed skill path provided by Codex.
- Claude Code: use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/icon-design`.
- Repository checkout: use `plugins/icon-design/skills/icon-design`.

Never write project output into an installed plugin or skill directory. It may be an installation cache. Create or use a separate working project directory.

## Optional guided brief

Web route:

```text
https://icons.kalebtec.com/
```

Bundled local route:

```sh
python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/launch_brief_builder.py"
```

Print the local file URL without opening it:

```sh
python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/launch_brief_builder.py" --print-url
```

The builder has three brief steps:

1. Purpose: project, icon class, job, audience, metaphor territory, and things to avoid.
2. References: up to three original visual-language specimens.
3. Character: a Guided preset or six Custom 1–5 axes with a 64-landmark reference matrix.

The builder always requests 20 discovery directions and a complete final package. Optional size, background, palette, and handoff constraints are under “Customize delivery.” It stores the unfinished brief locally in the browser and can export text or JSON.

## Core production rule

Run new work in exactly three production stages:

1. Discovery.
2. Refinement.
3. Final.

The guided brief is preparation, not a fourth production stage. Stop after each discovery or refinement sheet. Do not silently choose a concept for the user.

## Project layout

```text
icon-project/
  brief.json
  discovery/
    d1/concepts.json
    d1/D1-01.svg ... D1-20.svg
    d1/discovery-d1.png
  refinement/
    r1/concepts.json
    r1/R1-01.svg ... R1-08.svg
    r1/refinement-r1.png
  final/
    source/design.json
    source/mark.svg
    brand-suite/
    <slug>-brand-suite.zip
```

Use `D<round>-<two-digit number>` for discovery and `R<round>-<two-digit number>` for refinement. Never recycle an ID within a project.

## Stage 1: discovery

Generate 20 genuinely different representations by default. Change the metaphor, silhouette, construction logic, negative-space move, and rhythm. Small styling changes to one drawing do not count as separate concepts.

Create one square monochrome `currentColor` SVG per concept and a `concepts.json` manifest:

```json
{
  "project": "Project name",
  "stage": "discovery",
  "round": 1,
  "count": 20,
  "selected_parent": null,
  "concepts": [
    {
      "id": "D1-01",
      "title": "Short direction name",
      "file": "D1-01.svg",
      "territory": "metaphor or construction territory",
      "note": "one sentence explaining the distinctive move"
    }
  ]
}
```

Render the numbered comparison sheet:

```sh
python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/render_concept_sheet.py" \
  path/to/discovery/d1/concepts.json \
  --output path/to/discovery/d1/discovery-d1.png
```

Open and inspect the PNG. Confirm the sheet contains large, reverse, and native-size views. Deliver it with a compact territory summary. Stop after the sheet and wait for one of these instructions:

```text
Refine D1-07.
Continue discovery from D1-07 and D1-12, but make the next round quieter.
Change the brief: avoid enclosed badges and explore a more open silhouette.
```

Continued discovery normally creates another 20 distinct directions with IDs `D2-01` through `D2-20`.

## Stage 2: refinement

Start from one explicit discovery selection. Generate eight variants by default. Preserve the core idea while varying proportion, topology, counter shape, stroke or fill strategy, corner language, and optical balance.

Use IDs `R1-01` through `R1-08`. Set `stage` to `refinement` and set `selected_parent` to the selected discovery ID in `concepts.json`. Render the same standard sheet:

```sh
python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/render_concept_sheet.py" \
  path/to/refinement/r1/concepts.json \
  --output path/to/refinement/r1/refinement-r1.png
```

Open and inspect the sheet. Explain only meaningful differences. Stop after the sheet and wait for a numbered choice:

```text
Finalize R1-04.
Refine R1-04 further with a larger counter and less lower-right weight.
Back to discovery.
```

Continued refinement uses `R2-*` and records the selected discovery or refinement parent.

## Stage 3: final

Begin only after an explicit user choice, or when the user supplies an already approved SVG and asks only for production cleanup and exports.

Build a simple square canonical vector master. Keep visible master fills and strokes as `currentColor`. Apply optical corrections and inspect every requested native size and background. Color, material, and depth belong in derived contexts, not the master.

Prepare:

```text
final/source/
  design.json
  mark.svg
```

Minimum `design.json` shape:

```json
{
  "name": "Product name",
  "slug": "product-name",
  "aria_label": "Product name",
  "description": "A compact mark for Product name.",
  "story": "The visual idea and why the geometry supports it.",
  "source_svg": "mark.svg",
  "palette": {
    "ink": "#202124",
    "paper": "#F8F9FA",
    "paper_bright": "#FFFFFF",
    "paper_shadow": "#DADCE0",
    "background_top": "#202124",
    "background_bottom": "#303134",
    "accent": "#4C8BF5"
  },
  "geometry": {
    "tile_radius_ratio": 0.22,
    "favicon_mark_scale": 0.78,
    "app_mark_scale": 0.78,
    "avatar_mark_scale": 0.86,
    "preview_margin_ratio": 0.098,
    "preview_radius_ratio": 0.181
  },
  "material": "glass",
  "license": {
    "mode": "all-rights-reserved",
    "holder": "Product name",
    "year": 2026
  }
}
```

Render and validate the suite:

```sh
python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/render_suite.py" \
  --source path/to/final/source \
  --output path/to/final/brand-suite

python3 "<skill-directory>/scripts/validate_suite.py" \
  path/to/final/brand-suite \
  --zip path/to/final/<slug>-brand-suite.zip
```

`render_suite.py` also accepts `--zip PATH` or `--no-zip`. The standard flow creates the neighboring reproducible zip.

Install rendering dependencies outside the deliverable if needed:

```sh
python3 -m pip install -r "<skill-directory>/scripts/requirements.txt"
```

An isolated alternative is:

```sh
uv run --with cairosvg --with pillow python \
  "<skill-directory>/scripts/render_suite.py" \
  --source path/to/final/source \
  --output path/to/final/brand-suite
```

On Apple Silicon with Homebrew, if CairoSVG cannot find Cairo, prefix the render command with:

```sh
DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/lib
```

## Standard final package

The uncompressed suite and neighboring zip contain:

- Canonical `<slug>-mark.svg` using `currentColor`.
- Fixed `<slug>-mark-black.svg` and `<slug>-mark-white.svg`.
- Rounded-tile `<slug>-favicon.svg`.
- Multi-size `favicon.ico` plus 16, 32, and 48 px PNGs.
- `apple-touch-icon.png` at 180 px.
- Opaque `<slug>-app-icon-1024.png`.
- Transparent rounded `<slug>-app-icon-preview-1024.png`.
- Opaque circle-crop-safe `<slug>-avatar-512.png`.
- Flat Icon Composer background and transparent mark layers.
- App-icon and favicon comparison sheets.
- `design.json`, `render.py`, `requirements.txt`, `README.md`, and `LICENSE`.

The zip has no extra top-level directory and must match the uncompressed suite exactly. Keep discovery, refinement, prompts, chat logs, local paths, temporary environments, and design-tool screenshots outside it.

## Drawing rules

Translate the brief through:

```text
brand or task -> useful metaphor -> basic shapes -> distinctive move
```

- Research conventions and obvious competitors when web access is available. Use research to avoid collisions, never to trace or remix another mark. Do not claim trademark clearance.
- Start with meaning. For UI icons, prefer a learned metaphor. For brand marks, distill purpose, audience, personality, and memorable tension.
- Use a square grid, explicit safe area, repeated dimensions, a small anchor-point count, and a coherent corner and angle system.
- Use ratios only when they clarify relationships. Do not add ornamental construction circles after the fact.
- Check the outside silhouette and largest counters before polishing details.
- Test square, rounded-square, circular, light, dark, muted, and selected contexts as relevant.
- Inspect 16, 20, 24, 32, and 48 px outputs when relevant. Protect counters and critical gaps. Draw an explicit small-size optical variant if simple scaling fails.
- Use at least 3:1 contrast for meaningful graphical objects. Do not use color as the only state signal.
- Pair ambiguous workflow icons with text. Give meaningful UI icons accessible names and hide decorative icons from the accessibility tree.

## Master SVG rules

The canonical master must have:

- A square `viewBox`.
- Visible paint expressed as `currentColor`.
- No embedded bitmap.
- No external reference.
- No script.
- No text object.
- No decorative filter.
- No unnecessary editor metadata or transforms.

Preserve an editable construction source before expanding strokes or merging paths.

## Visual and automated validation

Visual checks are mandatory. Source inspection is not a visual check.

Open every comparison sheet and inspect 16 and 32 px rasters at native size. Confirm:

- The metaphor and silhouette survive.
- Counters stay open.
- The mark is optically centered in square and circular crops.
- Tile radius and mark scale feel related.
- The light mark has at least 3:1 contrast against both background endpoints.
- The app master is opaque.
- Rounded previews have transparent corners.
- The Composer mark layer is flat and transparent outside the mark.
- Fixed SVG variants contain no `currentColor`.
- The canonical SVG contains no embedded image, script, or external reference.

Automated validation checks structure and file formats; it cannot decide whether an icon is good.

## Existing SVG, audit, and direct-final routes

- Existing approved SVG plus cleanup/export request: skip discovery and refinement, audit the master, then run final.
- `Refine <ID>`: locate that concept and begin or continue refinement.
- `Finalize <ID>`: promote that exact concept to final.
- New metaphor or story: return to discovery.
- Style change within a selected idea: stay in refinement.
- Icon-family audit: compare grid, weight, corners, angles, counters, optical size, native-size rendering, context, and accessibility.

## Handoff response

Report:

- Chosen concept ID.
- Final zip and uncompressed suite paths.
- Smallest verified size.
- Optical corrections made.
- Backgrounds and contexts checked.
- Any remaining limitation.

Keep notes factual. Do not invent testimonials, trademark clearance, validation results, or checks that were not run. Do not add tool attribution to generated brand assets or their README.

## Repository development and release commands

Run the standalone Solid app:

```sh
npm install
npm run dev
```

Build and preview:

```sh
npm run build
npm run preview
```

Run web tests and sync the single-file builder into the plugin:

```sh
npm run check
```

Run renderer and workflow tests:

```sh
uv run --with cairosvg --with pillow python -m unittest discover -s tests -v
```

Deploy the linked site to Netlify:

```sh
netlify build
netlify deploy --build --prod
```

## Authoritative links

- [Visual brief builder](https://icons.kalebtec.com/): Interactive three-step brief and prompt export.
- [Repository](https://github.com/kalebteccom/claude-icon-design): Plugin, source app, renderers, examples, and tests.
- [Agent guide](https://icons.kalebtec.com/llms.txt): This complete plaintext workflow.
- [Material Design 3 icon guidance](https://m3.material.io/styles/icons/designing-icons): Grid and optical sizing reference.
- [Adobe icon design, Part 1](https://adobe.design/ideas/how-to-design-effective-icons-part-1): Meaning and recognition reference.
- [Adobe icon design, Part 2](https://adobe.design/ideas/how-to-design-effective-icons-part-2): System and refinement reference.
- [Icons8 pixel-perfect icon guide](https://icons8.com/blog/articles/make-pixel-perfect-icons/): Small-size raster guidance.
- [Figma logo design guide](https://www.figma.com/resource-library/how-to-design-a-logo/): Brief-to-concept process reference.
- [Supercharged Studio logo design math](https://www.supercharged.studio/blog/mathematical-logo-design): Geometry and proportion reference.
